skill.name: transformers
skill.description: Guides users through Hugging Face Transformers workflows for inference, generation, tokenization, model loading, and fine-tuning.
skill.summary: Use Hugging Face Transformers for model inference, generation, tokenization, and fine-tuning.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, eval, weak crypto, and URL patterns, but review found documentation examples rather than executable skill code. The main residual risk is user-driven package installation, model downloads, authentication, and optional Hub publishing.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to fenced or inline documentation examples. They are not run by the skill automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative locations are markdown code blocks for installation, login, and Transformers examples. No wrapper or script executes these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: PyTorch Evaluation Mode Misclassified as eval
low_findings.description: The eval alert is model.eval(), which switches a PyTorch model to evaluation mode. It is not dynamic code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text explicitly discusses training versus evaluation mode. The call is a normal PyTorch API method, not eval on user-controlled input.

low_findings.title: User-Driven External Services Require Awareness
low_findings.description: The skill references Hugging Face tokens, remote image input, model downloads, and Hub publishing. These are expected workflows, but users should review data sensitivity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external interactions are documented user actions, not hidden network behavior. They can expose data if a user publishes private models or processes remote content carelessly.

content.value_statement: Transformer projects often require many APIs for loading, inference, tokenization, and fine-tuning. This skill gives focused guidance for common Hugging Face Transformers workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to install Transformers and common task dependencies.
Shows pipeline patterns for text, image, audio, and multimodal inference.
Guides model loading with device placement, precision, quantization, and configuration options.
Covers text generation controls such as sampling, beam search, and length settings.
Describes tokenization, batching, padding, truncation, and chat templates.
Outlines Trainer-based fine-tuning, evaluation, checkpointing, and Hub publishing workflows.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, but it does not include runnable automation scripts.
Many workflows require local Python packages, model downloads, and suitable CPU or GPU resources.
Private or gated models may require a Hugging Face token configured by the user.
Users must evaluate model licenses, data privacy, and deployment constraints separately.

use_cases.title: Prototype NLP Inference
use_cases.description: Select a pipeline, load a model, and test classification, question answering, summarization, or translation quickly.

use_cases.title: Prepare Custom Fine-Tuning
use_cases.description: Plan dataset preprocessing, Trainer arguments, metrics, checkpointing, and evaluation for a task-specific model.

use_cases.title: Inspect Model Deployment Options
use_cases.description: Compare device placement, precision, quantization, SafeTensors, ONNX export, and memory tradeoffs before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to solve [task] with Hugging Face Transformers. Recommend the right pipeline, model type, inputs, and expected output format.

prompt_templates.title: Load a Model Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me load [model name] for [task]. Include tokenizer setup, device placement, precision settings, and memory considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Design Fine-Tuning Steps
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a fine-tuning plan for [dataset] and [target labels]. Cover preprocessing, Trainer settings, metrics, checkpoints, and evaluation.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Production Inference
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my transformer inference workflow for [latency or memory goal]. Suggest batching, quantization, caching, format, and device changes.

output_examples.input: Build a sentiment analysis prototype for product reviews.
output_examples.output: Recommended a text-classification pipeline, a sentiment model, batching guidance, and checks for label mapping and confidence scores.

output_examples.input: Plan fine-tuning for a five-class support ticket classifier.
output: Defined dataset split and tokenization steps.
Selected sequence classification model loading settings.
Outlined Trainer arguments, accuracy metrics, checkpointing, and evaluation review.

output_examples.input: Reduce memory use for a large causal language model.
output_examples.output: Suggested lower precision, quantization, automatic device mapping, smaller batch sizes, and local cache management.

best_practices: Specify model IDs explicitly so results do not change when library defaults change.
Use SafeTensors and review model licenses before loading third-party weights.
Keep private tokens and datasets out of prompts, logs, and public Hub repositories.
anti_patterns: Do not publish fine-tuned models that may contain private training data.
Do not assume a GPU is available without checking memory and device placement.
Do not use remote model or image URLs without reviewing trust, privacy, and licensing.

faq.question: Does this skill run model training for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples that users can adapt in their own Python environment.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Do I need a Hugging Face token?
faq.answer: Some gated or private models require a token. Public models may work without authentication.

faq.question: Can it help with computer vision and audio models?
faq.answer: Yes. The references include image classification, object detection, speech recognition, and audio classification pipeline patterns.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found low risk. Static alerts were mostly documentation false positives, with normal user-driven network workflows.

faq.question: What should users check before production use?
faq.answer: Users should review model licenses, data privacy, hardware requirements, dependency versions, and output quality for their domain.
